jokes.text: The dog problem has been solved... there's this rabbi in my building
jokes.setup: Jerry announces he's solved a problem with authority
jokes.punchline: Solution involves getting a rabbi to talk to someone about their dog
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the rabbi character who becomes central to the episode's comedy

jokes.text: I will never understand the bathrooms in this country. Why is it that the stall doors do not come all the way down to the floor?
jokes.setup: George transitions abruptly from engagement discussion to bathroom complaints
jokes.punchline: Specific obsession with bathroom stall door height
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurotic fixation - gets worked up about minor bathroom design flaw

jokes.text: Maybe it's so you can see if there's someone in there... Well, as a backup system
jokes.setup: Jerry offers logical explanation for bathroom door design
jokes.punchline: George escalates with 'backup system' - treating minor convenience as engineering failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld escalation - mundane observation becomes absurd systematic critique

jokes.text: That's not a system. That's a complete breakdown of the system.
jokes.setup: George's mounting frustration with bathroom door logic
jokes.punchline: Declares bathroom design a 'complete breakdown of the system'
jokes.explanation: Peak George - takes trivial annoyance and treats it as societal collapse. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: the subject should resolve itself based on its own momentum
jokes.setup: Discussion about changing subjects
jokes.punchline: George applies pseudo-intellectual 'momentum' theory to conversation topics
jokes.explanation: George's pretentious pseudo-philosophy about conversation flow - perfect character specificity

jokes.text: How am I gonna do this? I'm engaged to this woman? She doesn't even like me.
jokes.setup: George's internal panic about his engagement
jokes.punchline: Admits his fiancée doesn't even like him
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-awareness combined with helplessness - pathetic but relatable

jokes.text: Oh, what, you think I wanna marry George?
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests Elaine is jealous of George's engagement
jokes.punchline: Elaine interprets this as wanting to marry George specifically
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - Elaine deliberately misinterprets Jerry's suggestion

jokes.text: Do you realize this is going to be on our permanent records? It can never be erased. It'll follow us wherever we go for the rest of our lives.
jokes.setup: Kramer discusses some unspecified incident
jokes.punchline: Treats minor infraction like life-ruining criminal record
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer paranoid escalation - minor issue becomes life-defining catastrophe

jokes.text: I could care less. I hope it is on our record. I'm just sorry they didn't lock me up.
jokes.setup: Kramer's paranoia about permanent records
jokes.punchline: Elaine takes opposite extreme - wishes for harsher punishment
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to Kramer's worry - Elaine's defiant overreaction in opposite direction

jokes.text: oftentimes in life there are problems and just as often there are solutions
jokes.setup: Rabbi offering wisdom to Elaine
jokes.punchline: Incredibly obvious, unhelpful platitude delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Rabbi as dispensing useless wisdom with great confidence

jokes.text: Have I ever been less than forthright? No, you haven't. Well, maybe you have. What do I know? I probably have. Of course I have. What am I talking about?
jokes.setup: Jerry asks George for honest opinion
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately undermines his own credibility in neurotic spiral
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry neurotic self-doubt spiral - starts confident, ends completely undermining himself

jokes.text: Extend the doors on the toilet stalls at Yankee Stadium all the way to the floor.
jokes.setup: Jerry's 'big idea' for George
jokes.punchline: It's the same bathroom door complaint George had earlier
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Jerry presents George's own complaint as brilliant new idea

jokes.text: I think it's fantastic. I think it's a fantastic idea... Well, I told it to Susan before and she didn't like it.
jokes.setup: Jerry enthusiastically endorses the bathroom door idea
jokes.punchline: George reveals Susan rejected the same idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Jerry's enthusiasm followed by George's deflation

jokes.text: It should resolve of its own volition... That's exactly what I said, but I used the word 'momentum.' 'Momentum,' same thing. Same thing.
jokes.setup: Jerry uses 'volition' for conversation flow theory
jokes.punchline: George connects it to his earlier 'momentum' comment - they bond over pseudo-intellectualism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Jerry and George's similar pretentious tendencies - they validate each other's nonsense

jokes.text: My God, I'm getting married in December. Do you know that? Yeah, I know. How am I gonna make December?
jokes.setup: George realizes wedding is approaching
jokes.punchline: Treats December like an impossible physical challenge
jokes.explanation: George's physical panic about timeline - treats wedding date like marathon he can't train for

jokes.text: My stomach aches. My neck is killing me. I can't turn. Look, look... You're turning. No, that's not a good turn.
jokes.setup: George claims neck injury from wedding stress
jokes.punchline: Jerry observes he IS turning, George insists it's inadequate turning
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy - George's neurotic specificity about quality of neck turning

jokes.text: March 21st, the first day of spring... Rejuvenation, rebirth, everything's blooming. All that crap.
jokes.setup: George presents romantic spring wedding concept
jokes.punchline: Undermines own romantic pitch with 'all that crap'
jokes.explanation: Classic George - builds up romantic concept then deflates it with cynical dismissal

jokes.text: I think I'm a little bit scared of her. She's 5'3", like 100 pounds, I'm frightened to death of her.
jokes.setup: George admits fear of Susan
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes how physically small and non-threatening she is
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke - George's fear versus Susan's non-intimidating physical description

jokes.text: Well, she's a woman. They don't like to be disappointed. Especially her. She does not like disappointment.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains women's relationship to disappointment
jokes.punchline: Escalates to Susan specifically being extra intolerant of disappointment
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor with escalation to specific character

jokes.text: You know what, even if you killed somebody, I wouldn't turn you in.
jokes.setup: George expressing loyalty to Jerry
jokes.punchline: Uses extreme hypothetical of murder to show friendship
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the loyalty discussion that follows - George's extreme gesture of friendship

jokes.text: Definitely... I would turn you in... I wouldn't even think about it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks Kramer about turning him in for murder
jokes.punchline: Kramer's immediate, emphatic willingness to betray Jerry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer response - no hesitation about betraying friend, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Well, what kind of person are you, going around killing people? Well, if you killed this person, who's to say I wouldn't be next?
jokes.setup: Jerry argues Kramer should show loyalty
jokes.punchline: Kramer's paranoid logic about becoming next victim
jokes.explanation: Great Kramer paranoid logic - turns friendship loyalty into self-preservation

jokes.text: I have the SnackWells, which are very popular although I think sometimes with the so-called fat-free cookies people may overindulge, forgetting that they may be high in calories.
jokes.setup: Rabbi offers snacks to Elaine
jokes.punchline: Launches into unnecessary lecture about SnackWells calories
jokes.explanation: Good character bit - Rabbi's tendency to over-explain mundane things with authority

jokes.text: very often we cannot see the forest for the trees... Yeah, I don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Rabbi offers classic wisdom metaphor
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt admission she doesn't understand the cliché
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of pretentious wisdom - Elaine's honest confusion cuts through cliché

jokes.text: it should've been me. You know? I'm smart. I'm attractive.
jokes.setup: Elaine explaining jealousy about George's engagement
jokes.punchline: Listing her qualifications like a job interview
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Elaine's desperate self-promotion to a rabbi

jokes.text: So you're nothing but a stoolie. Admit it.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion about turning friends in
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dramatic accusation using old-fashioned 'stoolie'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to loyalty discussion with period-appropriate insult

jokes.text: Hey, don't do the crime if you can't do the time.
jokes.setup: Jerry calls Kramer a stoolie
jokes.punchline: Kramer responds with clichéd tough-guy saying
jokes.explanation: Relies on cliché but fits Kramer's character of using inappropriate tough talk

jokes.text: Hey, baby, I set the trends. Who do you think started this caffe latte thing? I've been drinking caffe lattes since the fifth grade and haven't looked back.
jokes.setup: Jerry questions Kramer's trendy drink choice
jokes.punchline: Kramer claims he invented caffe lattes and drank them as a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer delusion - claiming to have invented trendy coffee drinks as a child

jokes.text: I was supposed to see this five years ago. I was in a Chinese restaurant with George and Elaine. We got screwed up and missed it.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentions wanting to see Plan 9 from Outer Space
jokes.punchline: References previous Seinfeld episode where they missed the movie
jokes.explanation: Clever self-reference to famous earlier episode - reward for longtime viewers

jokes.text: See, that's littering. Maybe you ought to call the cops and turn me in. Maybe I will.
jokes.setup: Jerry drops paper, Kramer calls it littering
jokes.punchline: Jerry sarcastically suggests Kramer turn him in, Kramer considers it
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the stoolie theme - Kramer's consistency about turning people in

jokes.text: The Lauers? Really? Do you not wanna go? No, no. I wanna go.
jokes.setup: Susan mentions dinner plans with the Lauers
jokes.punchline: George's awkward over-enthusiasm masks clear reluctance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's inability to be honest about preferences

jokes.text: Spring! Rejuvenation, rebirth, everything is blooming. All that... If you don't wanna marry me, George, just say so!
jokes.setup: George repeats his spring wedding pitch
jokes.punchline: Susan immediately sees through it as postponement attempt
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to George's spring wedding scheme - Susan cuts through his romantic nonsense

jokes.text: No, no, still marry. Still marry... No, no, still love. Still love.
jokes.setup: Susan accuses George of not wanting to marry
jokes.punchline: George's panicked caveman-like reassurances
jokes.explanation: Perfect George panic mode - reduced to primitive reassurances. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Snow, Santa, all that stuff.
jokes.setup: George accepts Christmas wedding
jokes.punchline: Mirrors his earlier 'all that crap' about spring but with different tone
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier 'all that crap' - shows George's defeated state

jokes.text: She cried and you caved. How did you know that? I live and breathe, my friend. I live and breathe.
jokes.setup: Jerry correctly guesses what happened with Susan
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dramatic 'I live and breathe' as explanation for his wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry moment - his pretentious explanation for basic human insight

jokes.text: That's why breakups take two or three tries. You gotta build up your immunity. You see those tears, you don't know what to do. It's like she was on fire. I was just trying to put her out.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains the difficulty of breaking up
jokes.punchline: Compares woman's tears to fire emergency requiring immediate action
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor with vivid metaphor - tears as fire emergency

jokes.text: Well, at least you probably had some pretty good make-up sex afterwards. I didn't have any sex.
jokes.setup: Jerry assumes George got make-up sex
jokes.punchline: George's pathetic admission he got nothing
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing George's pathetic situation gets even worse

jokes.text: In your situation the only sex you're gonna have better than make-up sex is if you're sent to prison and you have a conjugal visit.
jokes.setup: George complaining about missing make-up sex
jokes.punchline: Jerry suggests prison conjugal visit as only remaining sexual hope
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant escalation - Jerry's extreme solution to George's sexual drought

jokes.text: Yeah, conjugal-visit sex. That is happening.
jokes.setup: Jerry's prison conjugal visit suggestion
jokes.punchline: George's enthusiastic acceptance of this terrible plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect George response - immediately considers prison as viable option

jokes.text: Look at this guy. He's eating a sandwich. Are you gonna eat those fries?
jokes.setup: Kramer observing someone in apparent distress
jokes.punchline: Focuses on food opportunities instead of showing concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer priorities - sees distressed person, thinks about free food

jokes.text: In fact, she told me that she wishes she was the one who was getting married... She came off as pretty desperate.
jokes.setup: Rabbi gossiping about Elaine to Jerry
jokes.punchline: Rabbi's blunt assessment of Elaine as 'desperate'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Rabbi's inappropriate gossiping with brutal honesty

jokes.text: I recall the word 'loser' peppered throughout our conversation.
jokes.setup: Rabbi revealing more of Elaine's private thoughts
jokes.punchline: Uses 'peppered throughout' for the word 'loser' - formal language for crude gossip
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - Rabbi's elevated diction describing Elaine's trash talk

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. Yes, of course, of course.
jokes.setup: George finally asks to postpone wedding to March
jokes.punchline: Susan's suspiciously easy agreement suggests manipulation
jokes.explanation: Ominous moment - Susan's quick agreement feels like a trap being set

jokes.text: But he's a rabbi. How can a rabbi have such a big mouth? That's what's so fascinating.
jokes.setup: Elaine outraged at Rabbi's gossip betrayal
jokes.punchline: Jerry's intellectual curiosity about the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Great contrast - Elaine's outrage vs Jerry's detached fascination with the anomaly

jokes.text: Because they don't allow outside drinks into the movie. Well, that's stupid. That's the rule. Yeah, well, we'll just see if we can't get around that.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains movie theater drink policy
jokes.punchline: Kramer's confident plan to circumvent simple rule
jokes.explanation: Good setup for upcoming coffee smuggling disaster - Kramer's overconfidence

jokes.text: I seem to recall a conversation with Mrs. Winston in 1F... I happened to mention to her how you felt it was never going to 'happen' for you.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if Rabbi told anyone else
jokes.punchline: Rabbi reveals more gossip recipients with painful details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rabbi's gossip spree - each revelation worse than the last

jokes.text: Well, this morning I happened to find myself in the elevator with him... Oh, my God, you didn't.
jokes.setup: Rabbi mentions elevator encounter with attractive guy
jokes.punchline: Elaine's horror at realizing he gossiped to her crush
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Rabbi has destroyed Elaine's romantic prospects

jokes.text: Come on, coffee boy.
jokes.setup: Kramer caught smuggling coffee into theater
jokes.punchline: Dismissive 'coffee boy' nickname for the arrested Kramer
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to coffee smuggling setup - Kramer's plan backfires completely

jokes.text: That man in no way represents our ability to take in a nice piece of juicy gossip and keep it to ourselves.
jokes.setup: Jerry defending Jewish people after Rabbi's gossip
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits Jews are good at gossiping while trying to defend them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-undermining defense - Jerry accidentally confirms the stereotype while denying it

jokes.text: I have seen the changes in you the past couple of years. Man, you have grown. You've matured... Well, I guess I'm getting older.
jokes.setup: Elaine's over-the-top congratulations to George
jokes.punchline: George's deadpan response deflating her elaborate praise
jokes.explanation: Good deflation - George's simple honesty undercuts Elaine's fake enthusiasm

jokes.text: Well, I started to tell her, and then all of a sudden, for some reason I just burst into tears. I bawled uncontrollably.
jokes.setup: George reporting back on postponement attempt
jokes.punchline: George broke down crying instead of being assertive
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - complete emotional breakdown when trying to be assertive

jokes.text: I never realized how powerful these tears are. I could have postponed it another five years if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: George's tears got him the postponement
jokes.punchline: George discovers tears as manipulation tool, considers extreme extension
jokes.explanation: Great George discovery - accidentally finds emotional manipulation works better than logic

jokes.text: You did me the biggest favour of my life. I spoke to a lawyer. We're suing for millions.
jokes.setup: Jerry apologizes for coffee incident
jokes.punchline: Kramer sees it as lawsuit opportunity worth millions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer opportunism - turns embarrassing arrest into get-rich-quick scheme

jokes.text: The coffee was too hot. It's supposed to be hot. Not that hot.
jokes.setup: Explanation for million-dollar lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Kramer's logic about coffee temperature degrees
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - Kramer's precise but nonsensical coffee temperature standards

jokes.text: A young lady I know, let's call her Elaine happened to find herself overwhelmed with feelings of resentment and hostility for her friend let's call him George.
jokes.setup: Rabbi giving sermon on TV
jokes.punchline: Uses Elaine and George's private business as sermon material with transparent pseudonyms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Rabbi's gossip reaches its peak by becoming public sermon material. Peak cringe comedy.

jokes.text: She also happened to mention to me that her friend had wondered if going to a prostitute while you're engaged is considered cheating. His feeling was they're never going to see each other again so, what's the difference?
jokes.setup: Rabbi continuing his sermon about 'George'
jokes.punchline: Reveals George's prostitute question with his rationalization on television
jokes.explanation: Peak episode moment - Rabbi broadcasts George's most embarrassing secret with his twisted logic on TV. Perfect escalation and character destruction. This approaches the 'three vasectomies' level of brilliant character-specific escalation.
